description: Wave 11 - 'Make Wave 9/10 Real' (DRAFT, not run). Padrino is backend-mature but product-surface-lagging: Wave 9 (human-MP, US-121..170), v9-fixes-2/3 (US-189..213), and Wave 10 (roles + NAR + multi-context rating, US-171..223) are all MERGED to main (HEAD 7e1dc45, Alembic head 0048). A holistic review found the engine grew far ahead of what is surfaced, runnable, or ranked. This wave is SINGLE-HOST and deliberately EXCLUDES campaign orchestration, multi-host/distributed workers, concurrent multi-gauntlet scheduling, and any large-scale cost-overhaul (there is no near-term benchmark campaign). Four phases. P0 (PRIMARY) - surface catch-up: drive the night-action UI from the SERVER's full legal-action set (the play surface hardcodes ['INVESTIGATE','PROTECT','MAFIA_KILL'] in web/dashboard/src/lib/api/playSurface.ts + src/routes/play/[gameId]/+page.svelte, forcing every NAR-role human seat to time out); render NAR resolution events in the spectator watch view (web/dashboard/src/routes/watch/[id]/+page.svelte renders only chat/vote/elimination/terminal today, NOT NightResolved/NightFeedbackDelivered) identity-blind; surface the multi-context leaderboard (canonical/PLACEMENT/SOLO_RATE cards already computed by ratings/public_leaderboard.py but the /leaderboard page is not linked in nav and FACTION cards are not shown); surface FACTION (Town vs Scum) ELO (persisted scope_type=FACTION but only GLOBAL is shown) and generalize the model rollup (model_rollup.py hardcodes Faction.TOWN/MAFIA); surface the six Wave-10 rulesets (deception13_v1, roleblock10_v1, visit12_v1, ninja13_v1, sk12_v1, jester8_v1) in the lobby (lobby/+page.svelte hardcodes only mini7_v1|bench10_v1). P1 - make the human lane RUNNABLE single-host: add a human-lane worker docker-compose service (compose has postgres/bootstrap/api/public-api/scheduler/dashboard only - no 'padrino human-lane' worker, so launched lobbies sit RUNNING forever) + a healthz probe; ops-size the placeholder caps/breakers (settings $50 global breaker padrino_human_global_lobby_cost_breaker_usd trips at real concurrency); fix per-call cost extraction for custom OpenAI-compatible endpoints (litellm_adapter._extract_cost reads _hidden_params['response_cost'] which is absent for Z.AI/Xiaomi so cost_usd is NULL - the price_turn_usd token fallback exists in economics but is not wired into the persist path); validate the existing single-host durable rehydration (runner/human_durability.rehydrate_active_human_games) is restart-resume sufficient (do NOT build distributed leasing). P2 - activate the ranked Humans-Included League (segregation-safe): a ranked-ELO writer for the HUMANS_INCLUDED league that PROVABLY never writes scientific Rating/RatingEvent (the human_rating sibling tables have been dormant since migration 0025 and human games are hardcoded ranked=False at human_lane.py:932; lobbies.py always uses get_or_create_humans_included which is ranked=False); extend tests/ratings/test_segregation.py to assert a RANKED human game writes ZERO scientific rows AND DOES write Humans-Included rows while a casual human game writes neither; surface human personal ELO + human/AI ladders on the multi-context leaderboard; address ranked integrity (out-of-band coaching guard + ranked anonymity timing-delay validation). P2 DEPENDS on P0 (human NAR-role play + leaderboard surface) and P1 (lane runnable). P3 - light single-host hardening (parallelizable): a real-action NAR golden corpus (tests/integration/test_nar_regression_corpus.py byte-pins the six rulesets ONLY as all-NOOP DRAW games via NoopMockAdapter, so tier-ordered kill/protect/roleblock/ninja/track/watch interactions have NO real-action golden) consolidating the drifting conftest script-builders; hot-path secondary indexes (game_events.game_id, llm_calls.(game_id, agent_build_id, event_id) + retention-scan columns) as one linear Alembic migration 0049 after head 0048; fix the non-isolating child-game gather in scheduler.py:254 (asyncio.gather with no return_exceptions aborts the whole gauntlet on one failure) + add a FAILED game status with a bounded single-host retry (no campaign abstraction); a single-host Postgres backup runbook + tested restore that re-verifies the hash-chain. Hard rules retained throughout: pure core (no wall-clock/unseeded-random/DB in core/), chat-firewall, identity-blind observations/spectator (FORBIDDEN_PAYLOAD_KEYS, counts-only composition, terminal-gated reveal), every event hash-chains, segregation (zero scientific writes for human games). DRAFT: refined to 21 stories (US-234 split into US-234a flag/plumbing + US-234b human-ranked-ELO writer). Not activated, not run.

userStories.title: Server-driven night-action play surface (drop the hardcoded action list)
userStories.description: The human play surface currently hardcodes the night-action universe in two places: web/dashboard/src/lib/api/playSurface.ts isNightActionPhase() tests ['MAFIA_KILL','PROTECT','INVESTIGATE'] and web/dashboard/src/routes/play/[gameId]/+page.svelte nightActionType() iterates ['INVESTIGATE','PROTECT','MAFIA_KILL']. The server already streams the full legal-action set per seat: the SSE observation frame (SeatObservationFrame) carries legal_actions.allowed_action_types (computed by core/engine/legal_actions.legal_actions_for, the single source of truth, which already emits ROLEBLOCK/FRAME/TRACK/WATCH/CLEAN per role/phase). NAR-role human seats (Godfather/Roleblocker/Janitor/Framer/Tracker/Watcher/Ninja/SK) currently cannot act and are forced to time out. Drive the night-action UI entirely from allowed_action_types/legal_targets - no hardcoded action list anywhere in the frontend. This fixes a real human-MP bug.
acceptanceCriteria: isNightActionPhase() and nightActionType() in playSurface.ts and play/[gameId]/+page.svelte no longer reference any hardcoded list of action types; the night-action determination derives from the server's legal_actions.allowed_action_types (the single source of truth) for the streamed observation frame. MAFIA_KILL, PROTECT, INVESTIGATE remain renderable and submittable exactly as today (still members of allowed_action_types) - the change is to source ALL action types from the server's allowed_action_types, NOT to delete the vanilla three when removing the hardcoded array.

All NAR night ActionTypes the server can emit (ROLEBLOCK, FRAME, TRACK, WATCH, CLEAN, SERIAL_KILL) are renderable and submittable when allowed_action_types contains them, with targets from legal_targets; a frontend test (vitest/unit on playSurface) drives an observation whose allowed_action_types contains a NAR action and asserts the surface offers it.
A contract test asserts the streamed SeatObservationFrame / observation SSE carries allowed_action_types so the server-driven UI has the data end-to-end.

An e2e test (web/dashboard/tests/e2e, alongside play.spec.ts) drives a play observation whose legal_actions contains a non-vanilla night action and asserts the play surface presents that action and its legal targets rather than timing out.

The existing anonymity assertions in play.spec.ts still pass (the rendered play-shell exposes no seat_kind/is_human/agent_build/controller_type/takeover); no identity marker is introduced by the new action rendering.
pnpm -C web/dashboard lint && check && test:e2e pass.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Pure frontend wiring; the server contract (legal_actions_for + the SSE observation frame) is already correct. Single source of truth = core/engine/legal_actions.legal_actions_for. Real human-MP bug: NAR-role seats forced to time out.

userStories.title: Render NAR night-resolution events in the spectator watch view (identity-blind)
userStories.description: The spectator watch view web/dashboard/src/routes/watch/[id]/+page.svelte renders only PublicMessageSubmitted, VoteSubmitted/VoteCast, DayVoteResolved/PhaseResolved, PlayerEliminated, and GameTerminated. It does not render the night outcome. The ONLY spectator-PUBLIC night-resolution event is PlayerEliminated (visibility=PUBLIC, events.py:410); NightFeedbackDelivered is PRIVATE (events.py:347) and NightResolved is SYSTEM (events.py:392), and core/spectator_projection.py drops both wholesale (SPECTATOR_DROP_VISIBILITIES={PRIVATE,SYSTEM}, project_event_for_spectator returns None for any non-PUBLIC event). Add identity-blind renderers for the spectator-PUBLIC night outcome only: the night-death / no-death announcement carried by the projected PUBLIC PlayerEliminated payload, including the cleaned (Janitor) no-reveal variant, so a watcher can follow that a night resolved. The spectator projection is the source of truth for what is visible; per-seat private feedback (roleblock/protect/investigate/track/watch codes) is NEVER projected to the public watch stream and must not be rendered. Richer public night narration would require a NEW PUBLIC spectator night-summary event in core and is OUT OF SCOPE for this wave.
acceptanceCriteria: The watch route renders the spectator-PUBLIC night outcome - the night-death / no-death announcement from the projected PUBLIC PlayerEliminated payload (including the cleaned (Janitor) no-reveal variant) - using ONLY fields present in the spectator projection (core/spectator_projection.py); it never reads role/faction/model/provider/human-vs-AI from the frame. It does NOT attempt to render roleblock/protect/investigate/track/watch outcomes (those ride NightFeedbackDelivered=PRIVATE / NightResolved=SYSTEM and are dropped by the projection).

A backend test asserts project_event_for_spectator returns None for every NightFeedbackDelivered event and every NightResolved event, so per-seat private codes (INVESTIGATION_RESULT/TRACK_RESULT/WATCH_RESULT/ACTION_BLOCKED) and the SYSTEM night summary never enter the watch feed.

Identity-blindness holds: a watch e2e test (web/dashboard/tests/e2e/watch.spec.ts) on a NAR game asserts the rendered watch view contains none of seat_kind/is_human/agent_build/controller_type/takeover and no role/faction strings before the terminal reveal; reuse the shared identity-blind assertion helper (see US-225's shared-helper AC).

A shared identity-blind e2e assertion helper is created extending the existing play.spec.ts/lobby.spec.ts anonymity pattern, so play + watch + leaderboard specs assert identity-blindness through one helper rather than drifting per-story copies.

Counts-only / terminal-gated rules are respected: cleaned (Janitor) deaths do not reveal the cleaned role mid-game in the watch view, and role/faction only appear via the terminal endgame reveal (core/reveal.py) path the reveal.spec.ts gate already covers.
The PlayerEliminated night-outcome renderer is exercised by the watch e2e fixture (a scripted/fixture NAR game) so it is covered by the anonymity gate.
pnpm -C web/dashboard lint && check && test:e2e pass.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Source of truth for visibility = core/spectator_projection.py (PRIVATE/SYSTEM dropped, FORBIDDEN_PAYLOAD_KEYS stripped). The only spectator-PUBLIC night-resolution event is PlayerEliminated (events.py:410); NightFeedbackDelivered (PRIVATE, events.py:347) and NightResolved (SYSTEM, events.py:392) are dropped wholesale - DO NOT try to render roleblock/protect/investigate/track/watch from them. Terminal reveal stays in reveal.py. Richer public night narration needs a NEW PUBLIC core event = out of scope.

userStories.title: Surface the six Wave-10 rulesets in the lobby selector (no hardcoded ruleset list)
userStories.description: web/dashboard/src/routes/lobby/+page.svelte hardcodes the ruleset to a 'mini7_v1' | 'bench10_v1' union with two static <option>s. The six Wave-10 rulesets (deception13_v1, roleblock10_v1, visit12_v1, ninja13_v1, sk12_v1, jester8_v1) are registered in core/rulesets/__init__.py (BUILTIN_RULESET_IDS) and resolvable via get_ruleset but cannot be selected to create a lobby. Surface the available rulesets from the server rather than a hardcoded union, so newly-registered rulesets appear without a frontend edit. Add (or reuse) an API that lists the builtin ruleset ids + display metadata, and render them in the lobby selector (and any home/ladder selection that hardcodes the union).
acceptanceCriteria: An API endpoint returns the list of selectable ruleset ids with display metadata (player count / label), sourced from get_ruleset/BUILTIN_RULESET_IDS - never a hardcoded route-level list (hard rule 5); a backend route test asserts all six Wave-10 ruleset ids plus mini7_v1/bench10_v1 are returned.

lobby/+page.svelte populates its ruleset selector from that endpoint (or a typed server-provided list) instead of the hardcoded 'mini7_v1' | 'bench10_v1' union; an e2e test (lobby.spec.ts) asserts a Wave-10 ruleset id is selectable.
The lobby/home/ladder selection no longer pins a literal two-ruleset list; adding a ruleset to BUILTIN_RULESET_IDS surfaces it without further frontend edits (assert via the populated selector).
Existing lobby anonymity assertions (roster exposes no seat_kind/is_human/agent_build, composition is counts-only) still pass.
pnpm -C web/dashboard lint && check && test:e2e pass.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Source of truth = core/rulesets/__init__.py get_ruleset/BUILTIN_RULESET_IDS. Do not hardcode rulesets in routes/lobby (hard rule 5). Check whether a ruleset-list endpoint already exists before adding one.

userStories.title: Generalize the per-faction model rollup beyond Town/Mafia
userStories.description: FACTION-scope ELO is already persisted (Rating.scope_type='FACTION', scope_value in {TOWN, MAFIA}) but ratings/model_rollup.rollup_by_model hardcodes Faction.TOWN.value / Faction.MAFIA.value: _faction_aggregate branches on TOWN vs (else) MAFIA, ModelLeaderboardEntry has dedicated town/mafia fields, and the entry construction calls _faction_aggregate twice with the two literals. Generalize rollup_by_model to iterate whatever FACTION scope_values are present in the persisted FACTION-scope rating rows so a future 3rd-faction writer needs no rollup edit. NOTE: only the binary winner's FACTION rows (scope_value in {TOWN, MAFIA}) are written today (ratings/openskill_service.py); solo/3rd factions (e.g. SK from sk12_v1) flow through the PLACEMENT context, NOT FACTION, so no FACTION row for a solo faction exists yet - the >2-faction test therefore feeds a SYNTHETIC FACTION-scope fixture explicitly labeled forward-compat (not produced by the current writer). Keep the canonical 2-faction numbers byte-identical.
acceptanceCriteria: rollup_by_model no longer hardcodes Faction.TOWN.value/Faction.MAFIA.value in _faction_aggregate and the entry construction; per-faction aggregates are produced by iterating the faction values actually present in the persisted FACTION-scope rating rows (faction-keyed dict).
A regression test asserts the rollup output for an existing Town/Mafia dataset is byte-identical (same mu/sigma/conservative_score/games/wins per faction) before/after the generalization.

A test feeds a SYNTHETIC >2-faction FACTION-scope fixture (a third faction's FACTION-scope rows, explicitly labeled forward-compat: not produced by the current writer) and asserts all factions appear in the rollup without a code change.
Pure aggregation; no wall-clock/unseeded-random.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Hardcoded sites: ratings/model_rollup.py rollup_by_model + _faction_aggregate (TOWN vs else-MAFIA) + ModelLeaderboardEntry town/mafia fields. openskill_service.py emits FACTION-scope rows ONLY for the binary winner (scope_value in {TOWN, MAFIA}); SK/solo outcomes go through PLACEMENT, not FACTION - so a >2-faction FACTION dataset is necessarily synthetic forward-compat. This is read/aggregation generalization only; does not change rating WRITES. Backend-only (feeds US-228).

userStories.title: Surface FACTION (Town vs Scum) ELO cards in the public leaderboard API
userStories.description: FACTION-scope rating rows are persisted by ratings/openskill_service.py (SCOPE_FACTION, scope_value TOWN/MAFIA) but ratings/public_leaderboard.py never queries them - _canonical_cards/_placement_cards are GLOBAL-only (L607-608, L662-663). This story ADDS a new FACTION-scope query + cards (Town vs Scum, and any generalized faction from US-227); it does not expose an existing internal computation. Surface per-faction ELO cards in the public leaderboard contract so the dashboard can show faction asymmetry. Keep the hard canonical-vs-experimental section separation and never co-sort across scopes/contexts.
acceptanceCriteria: compute_public_leaderboard surfaces FACTION-scope cards (per ruleset) in addition to GLOBAL, as their own labeled cards with scope_type/scope_value, provisional/min-sample flags carried through; FACTION cards are never co-sorted with GLOBAL or with non-canonical context cards.

The /public/leaderboard response (api/routes/public.py PublicLeaderboardResponse) includes the FACTION cards with distinct scope labels; an API route test asserts a model with Town and Mafia rows yields separate Town/Mafia cards plus its GLOBAL card.

Canonical-vs-experimental separation is preserved (FACTION cards of a CANONICAL_TEAM context stay in the canonical section; PLACEMENT/SOLO_RATE stay experimental); a test asserts no merged/co-sorted list across contexts.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Backend read-aggregation only - this ADDS a new FACTION-scope query (public_leaderboard.py references FACTION only via the imported constant today; both card queries pin SCOPE_GLOBAL/SCOPE_VALUE_GLOBAL at L607-608, L662-663). Sites: ratings/public_leaderboard.py _canonical_cards/_placement_cards (GLOBAL-only today) + api/routes/public.py PublicLeaderboardResponse. Feeds the frontend leaderboard (US-229).

userStories.title: Multi-context leaderboard in the nav: canonical + PLACEMENT + SOLO_RATE + FACTION sections
userStories.description: The /leaderboard page (web/dashboard/src/routes/leaderboard/+page.svelte) already renders canonical_cards and experimental_cards but is NOT linked in the main nav (web/dashboard/src/routes/+layout.svelte links Home/Ladder/Play only), and it does not render the FACTION cards added by US-228. Surface the full multi-context leaderboard in the nav/home: canonical cards, PLACEMENT cards, and SOLO_RATE cards in clearly SEPARATE sections, never co-sorted, with provisional/min-sample flags shown; add the FACTION (Town vs Scum) cards as their own sub-section. Link it in nav and from home.
acceptanceCriteria: The leaderboard page renders canonical, PLACEMENT, and SOLO_RATE cards in visually distinct, separately-headed sections that are never merged into one sorted list; provisional/min-sample (under-sampled) models are explicitly labeled, not ranked.

FACTION cards are distinguished in the frontend solely via the scope_type/scope_value fields already carried on PublicRatingCardResponse (scope_type=='FACTION'); group by scope_type, never infer faction from any identity/role string. Scope the FACTION sub-section to the two persisted scope_values (Town vs Scum); generalized >2-faction cards (US-227) surface only when present - do NOT require a 3rd-faction card in the e2e. A unit test asserts FACTION-scope cards render in a separate sub-section from scope_type=='GLOBAL'.

The leaderboard is reachable from the main nav (+layout.svelte) and linked from the home page (+page.svelte); an e2e test navigates from home/nav to the leaderboard and asserts the section headers exist.

An e2e test (leaderboard.spec.ts) asserts canonical and experimental sections are not co-sorted and that a provisional flag renders when present; it reuses the shared identity-blind assertion helper (created in US-225) for the leaderboard surface.
pnpm -C web/dashboard lint && check && test:e2e pass.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: The page + the public_leaderboard API already exist; this extends presentation + nav linkage. Both reviewers: 'separate context' must be screamingly obvious; never co-sort.

userStories.title: Human-lane worker docker-compose service + healthz probe
userStories.description: docker-compose.yml defines postgres/bootstrap/api/public-api/scheduler/dashboard only - there is NO human-lane worker service, so the 'padrino human-lane' worker (cli.py human-lane -> runner/human_lane.run_human_lane) never runs in the compose stack and launched human lobbies sit RUNNING forever. Add a human-lane worker compose service that runs 'padrino human-lane' against the same postgres, gated on bootstrap completion like the other workers, internal-network-only, with a liveness/health probe. Mirror the scheduler service's depends_on/restart posture.
acceptanceCriteria: docker-compose.yml gains a human-lane worker service running the 'padrino human-lane' command against postgres, depends_on bootstrap service_completed_successfully + postgres healthy, internal-network-only (no published port), with a restart policy matching scheduler.

A health/liveness probe exists for the worker (e.g. a /healthz/human-lane endpoint queried like the scheduler's /healthz/scheduler, or an equivalent worker liveness check); a test asserts the probe endpoint/handler exists and reports lane liveness.
A compose-shape test (or a parse-and-assert test of docker-compose.yml) asserts the human-lane service exists with the expected command and dependency gating.
Document in progress.txt that the human lane is now part of the runnable single-host stack (no distributed worker).
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Entry point: cli.py 'human-lane' command -> runner/human_lane.run_human_lane(session_factory, concurrency, stop_event). No Redis (hard rule). Single-host only. Confirm/add a healthz route alongside the existing /healthz and /healthz/scheduler.

userStories.title: Ops-size the human-lane cost caps + global breaker (sensible + configurable)
userStories.description: The placeholder human-lane economics trip too early at real concurrency. settings.py defines padrino_human_global_lobby_cost_breaker_usd=50.0, padrino_human_lobby_cost_cap_usd=2.0, padrino_human_admission_inference_reserve_usd=0.5, and per-user/day caps; economics/human_cost_governance.global_breaker_open() opens once cumulative spend >= the $50 breaker. At real concurrency the global breaker trips almost immediately and STOPS new lobbies/turns. Re-size the placeholder caps to sensible single-host defaults and ensure every cap is configurable via settings/env (no magic numbers in the governance logic). Do NOT build a cost overhaul - just sane, overridable defaults + clear breaker-open observability.
acceptanceCriteria: Re-size single-host defaults in settings.py (e.g. padrino_human_global_lobby_cost_breaker_usd raised from 50.0 to a documented single-host value); document the chosen numbers + rationale in progress.txt; a test asserts the new default and that an env override changes the breaker threshold.

A test asserts global_breaker_open() and the per-lobby/per-user caps read exclusively from Settings (overriding a setting changes the breaker threshold) and that the breaker only STOPS new lobbies/turns - it never kills an active game (assert active-game continuation when the breaker is open).
A test asserts the breaker-open path emits the existing structured warning with spent_usd/cap_usd so operators can see why new lobbies are refused.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Sites: settings.py (padrino_human_global_lobby_cost_breaker_usd etc.) + economics/human_cost_governance.global_breaker_open / per-lobby / per-user caps. Single-host scope; not a cost overhaul. Preserve the 'breaker never kills an active game' invariant already in the docstring.

userStories.title: Wire token-based cost fallback into the per-call cost persist path (custom endpoints)
userStories.description: llm/litellm_adapter._extract_cost reads response._hidden_params['response_cost'], which is absent for custom OpenAI-compatible endpoints (Z.AI/Xiaomi), so LlmCall.cost_usd is persisted NULL and the human-lane $ breaker / per-model cost silently sees zero. A token-based fallback already exists - economics/human_cost_governance.price_turn_usd(settings, response_cost, model, input_tokens, output_tokens) falls back to settings.padrino_human_fallback_token_price_per_1k when response_cost is None - but it is not wired into the cost computed at the adapter/persist boundary. Wire the fallback so cost_usd is never silently zero/NULL for a priced model, using token counts from the response.
acceptanceCriteria: The per-call cost written to LlmCall.cost_usd uses price_turn_usd (or an equivalent fallback) so that when litellm returns no response_cost, the token-based price table produces a non-null cost for a model present in padrino_human_fallback_token_price_per_1k; a test drives a custom-endpoint-style response with no _hidden_params response_cost and asserts cost_usd is the token-table value, not NULL/zero.
When response_cost IS present it is still preferred (test asserts the litellm value wins over the fallback).
The fallback price table is configurable via settings (a new model can be priced without code change); a test asserts an overridden table entry is used.

The human-lane global spend accounting (global_human_lane_spend_usd) reflects the fallback-priced turns so the breaker is not silently zero; a test asserts cumulative spend includes a fallback-priced turn.

Apply the price_turn_usd fallback at the PERSIST boundary where Settings + model + AdapterResult input/output token counts are all in scope - NOT inside _extract_cost. _extract_cost keeps returning litellm response_cost or None; price_turn_usd(settings, response_cost=..., model=..., input_tokens=..., output_tokens=...) resolves None to the token-table price. A test asserts the persisted LlmCall.cost_usd value, not _extract_cost in isolation.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Sites: the persist boundary (Settings+model+tokens in scope) wires price_turn_usd; llm/litellm_adapter._extract_cost (response-only, lines ~377-383, called at L315) keeps returning response_cost or None - do NOT thread Settings into it. economics/human_cost_governance.price_turn_usd (the fallback already exists, prefers response_cost when present, L110), settings.padrino_human_fallback_token_price_per_1k, db/models.LlmCall.cost_usd. The fix is wiring at the persist seam, not net-new math.

userStories.title: Validate single-host human-lane restart-resume (durable rehydration) - no distributed leasing
userStories.description: The human lane already has durable rehydration: runner/human_durability.rehydrate_active_human_games() replays the hash-chained game_events to rebuild GameState for in-progress human games on worker start (using human_game_runtime + the reconnect-grace presence concept, not a lease). For a SINGLE worker this is sufficient; the review asked to VALIDATE restart-resume and a single-host heartbeat sufficiency, NOT to build distributed leasing. Add an explicit restart-resume test proving a worker restart resumes an in-progress human game from the durable state with a continuous hash chain, and document that single-host requires no lease.
acceptanceCriteria: A test starts a human game, simulates a worker stop with the game in-progress, then calls rehydrate_active_human_games() (fresh worker) and asserts the game resumes from the persisted phase/deadline with the hash chain continuing unbroken (replay-equals-live for the resumed tail).
The test asserts no duplicate/double-processing of the in-progress phase on resume (idempotent rehydration).

A documented note (progress.txt) states the single-host lane needs no distributed lease; the reconnect-grace presence mechanism (padrino_human_reconnect_grace_seconds) is the only liveness concept and distributed leasing is explicitly out of scope.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Validate, do NOT build leasing. Sites: runner/human_durability.rehydrate_active_human_games, human_game_runtime table, runner/human_lane reconnect-grace (padrino_human_reconnect_grace_seconds). Depends on US-230 so the worker that does the resume is the one in the compose stack.

userStories.title: Ranked lobby flag end-to-end (frontend toggle + league binding + human_lane ranked plumbing; ranked game still writes NOTHING yet)
userStories.description: Human games are hardcoded casual: api/routes/lobbies.create_lobby always uses get_or_create_humans_included (ranked=False) and runner/human_lane passes ranked=False positionally into the game loop. This story wires the ranked flag end-to-end WITHOUT yet writing any ratings: a frontend ranked toggle on lobby creation, a ranked binding through lobbies.create_lobby (a ranked lobby binds a HUMANS_INCLUDED league with ranked=True; a casual lobby stays ranked=False), and the human_lane ranked plumbing so the ranked flag reaches the terminal-outcome path. A ranked game still writes NOTHING (no human_rating, no scientific rows) at the end of this story - the writer is US-234b. The canonical Rating/RatingEvent reach path (League.kind==SCIENTIFIC + _should_apply_ratings) is UNCHANGED.
acceptanceCriteria: Lobby creation accepts an opt-in ranked flag (default casual) from a frontend ranked toggle; a ranked lobby binds a HUMANS_INCLUDED league with ranked=True; a casual lobby stays ranked=False. A backend test asserts the ranked flag is persisted/bound on the league for a ranked lobby and absent for a casual lobby.

The human_lane ranked plumbing threads the ranked flag to the terminal-outcome path: runner/human_lane's execute closure calls drive_game_loop(config, adapter, False, ...) - the positional False at human_lane.py:929-937 (L932) is the ranked literal; the ranked param is already threaded at L678/L694 and the always-casual invariant is commented at L874. Replace the hardcoded positional with the bound ranked value so the terminal-outcome path sees the real ranked flag. A test asserts a ranked human game reaches the terminal-outcome path with ranked=True.

RANKED GAME STILL WRITES NOTHING in this story: a ranked HUMANS_INCLUDED terminal game writes ZERO scientific Rating/RatingEvent rows AND ZERO human_rating/human_rating_event rows (the writer is US-234b); a test asserts both counts are zero.
The canonical Rating/RatingEvent reach path (League.kind==SCIENTIFIC + _should_apply_ratings) is UNCHANGED; a test asserts a SCIENTIFIC game still writes scientific rows.
Anonymity preserved end-to-end: the ranked toggle introduces no identity leak in any observation/spectator surface (the existing FORBIDDEN_PAYLOAD_KEYS/anonymity gates still pass).
pnpm -C web/dashboard lint && check && test:e2e pass.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Hardcodes to flip: lobbies.create_lobby (get_or_create_humans_included ranked=False) + the positional False at human_lane.py:929-937 (L932; ranked param threaded at L678/L694, always-casual comment at L874). This story is the flag/plumbing only - the writer fires from the terminal-outcome path in US-234b, not by flipping the positional alone. Frontend touches the lobby ranked toggle, hence the pnpm gate.

userStories.title: Human-ranked-ELO writer for human_rating/human_rating_event (segregation-proof)
userStories.description: The human_rating/human_rating_event sibling tables (migration 0025) are dormant. With the ranked flag now wired end-to-end (US-234a), light up a NEW ranked-ELO writer that updates ONLY the human_rating/human_rating_event sibling tables (OpenSkill update keyed by human_player_id). It fires from the terminal-outcome path ONLY when League.kind==HUMANS_INCLUDED AND ranked. It must PROVABLY never write the scientific Rating/RatingEvent tables - reuse the fail-closed segregation posture (League.kind + the _should_apply_ratings chokepoint pattern) on a SEPARATE human reach path keyed by League.kind==HUMANS_INCLUDED, never on the canonical reach path.
acceptanceCriteria: A new human-ranked-ELO writer updates ONLY human_rating/human_rating_event for a ranked HUMANS_INCLUDED terminal game (OpenSkill update keyed by human_player_id, with scope_type/scope_value), fired from the terminal-outcome path ONLY when the league is HUMANS_INCLUDED AND ranked; a unit test asserts it writes human_rating rows and ZERO scientific rows.

human_rating/human_rating_event use scope_type=GLOBAL, scope_value=global ONLY in this wave (matching the dormant migration-0025 sibling tables and keeping US-236's read simple); FACTION/PLACEMENT human scopes are explicitly out of scope. A test asserts the writer emits only GLOBAL-scope human rating rows.

FAIL CLOSED: a missing/unknown ranked flag or league kind coerces to casual/unrated; the human writer can never be reached for a SCIENTIFIC league; a test asserts a SCIENTIFIC terminal game never invokes the human writer and a casual human game never invokes it either.
Anonymity preserved end-to-end: ranked human play introduces no identity leak in any observation/spectator surface (the existing FORBIDDEN_PAYLOAD_KEYS/anonymity gates still pass).
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Tables exist since 0025 (HumanRating/HumanRatingEvent), dormant. Reuse the _should_apply_ratings fail-closed pattern but on a SEPARATE human reach path keyed by League.kind==HUMANS_INCLUDED AND ranked, firing from the terminal-outcome path. GLOBAL-scope only this wave (matches the sibling-table heritage; US-236 reads it back).

userStories.title: Extend the segregation CI gate for the ranked vs casual human paths
userStories.description: tests/ratings/test_segregation.py currently asserts a casual human game writes zero scientific rows and that even an accidental ranked=True human seat fails closed (counts == (0,0,0,0)). Now that the ranked Humans-Included writer exists (US-234), the gate must assert the THREE-way invariant: a RANKED human game writes ZERO scientific/canonical rows AND DOES write Humans-Included (human_rating/human_rating_event) rows; a CASUAL human game writes NEITHER scientific NOR human_rating rows; a SCIENTIFIC game writes scientific rows and zero human_rating rows. This is the CI proof that lighting up ranked did not breach segregation.
acceptanceCriteria: test_segregation.py gains a test asserting a RANKED HUMANS_INCLUDED terminal game writes ZERO rows to ratings/rating_events AND writes >=1 row to human_rating and human_rating_event.
A test asserts a CASUAL human game writes ZERO rows to ratings/rating_events AND ZERO rows to human_rating/human_rating_event (casual is still fully dormant).
A test asserts a SCIENTIFIC ranked game writes scientific rating rows and ZERO human_rating rows (the inverse direction).
The gate runs in the default pytest -m 'not integration' suite and fails if any direction is violated.

Reuse the existing _count_all_rating_rows 4-tuple helper (ratings, rating_events, human_ratings, human_rating_events) and EXTEND the existing scientific test at tests/ratings/test_segregation.py:~L214 - do not add a parallel counter or rewrite the in-tree gate.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Builds directly on US-234b's writer. Existing helpers: _count_all_rating_rows returns the 4-tuple (ratings, rating_events, human_ratings, human_rating_events) at test_segregation.py:155-169; a ranked scientific league test already exists at ~L214. Extend the single-source gate, do not rewrite it.

userStories.title: Surface human personal ELO + human/AI ladders on the multi-context leaderboard
userStories.description: Once ranked human games write human_rating (US-234b) and the multi-context leaderboard is surfaced (US-229), expose human personal ELO and the human/AI ladders on the leaderboard. The Humans-Included League ladder is a SEPARATE context from the scientific canonical ladders and must never be co-sorted with them; human personal ELO is keyed by human_player_id and must stay identity-blind on any pre-reveal surface (no human display identity leaking into live/observation views).
acceptanceCriteria: The public leaderboard API surfaces Humans-Included League cards (human personal ELO + the human/AI ladder for ranked human games) as their own clearly-separated section, never co-sorted with scientific canonical/PLACEMENT/SOLO_RATE cards; provisional/min-sample flags carried through.

A backend test asserts the Humans-Included cards come from human_rating (not Rating) and are labeled as a distinct league/context. The read consumes the GLOBAL-scope human_rating rows that US-234b's writer emits (scope_type=GLOBAL, scope_value=global - the only human scope written this wave); FACTION/PLACEMENT human scopes are out of scope.
The leaderboard page renders the Humans-Included section distinctly; an e2e test asserts it is present and visually separated from the scientific sections.

Identity-blindness: nothing on this surface reveals which seats were human in any specific live/in-progress game; only aggregated post-game human ladder standings are shown (covered by the existing anonymity gates where applicable).
pnpm -C web/dashboard lint && check && test:e2e pass.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: This is the user's chosen ranked product surface. Depends on the P0 multi-context surface (US-229) and the P2 writer (US-234b). Reads back US-234b's GLOBAL-scope human_rating rows (GLOBAL-only this wave). Keep the human ladder its own hard-walled section.

userStories.title: Ranked-integrity guard: out-of-band coaching + ranked anonymity timing-delay validation
userStories.description: Making human games ranked turns out-of-band coaching (a human secretly fed reads by a stronger player/model outside the game) into an integrity problem, and ranked raises the stakes on the anonymity timing-delay holding. Add a concrete, enforceable ranked-integrity acknowledgment on ranked lobby creation (a boolean integrity_acknowledged flag, reusing the existing lobby record - NO new table, NO migration, NO collusion-detection or anti-coaching infrastructure) AND validate that the existing symmetric fixed release-delay (runner/human_tick.run_human_tick: padrino_human_release_delay_seconds applied identically to human and AI public messages) holds on the ranked path so response latency cannot leak human-vs-AI in a ranked game.
acceptanceCriteria: Ranked lobby creation requires a boolean integrity_acknowledged flag on the ranked lobby (reuse the existing lobby record / a boolean column - NO new table, NO migration, NO collusion-detection or anti-coaching infrastructure). A ranked lobby with integrity_acknowledged=false is rejected (422); a casual lobby ignores the flag. A test asserts: ranked-without-ack refused (422), ranked-with-ack persists the flag, casual creates without it.

A test asserts the symmetric fixed release-delay (all public messages share one released_at instant via run_human_tick) holds on the RANKED path exactly as on the casual path - human and AI messages release at the identical instant, so timing cannot out a seat in a ranked game.
No new identity leak is introduced on the ranked path (existing anonymity gates pass).
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Concrete guard: a reused boolean integrity_acknowledged on the ranked lobby record (no new table/migration, no anti-collusion infra); ranked-without-ack -> 422. The timing-delay validation is the load-bearing AC. Sites: runner/human_tick.run_human_tick (released_at symmetry), settings.padrino_human_release_delay_seconds, lobby creation (ranked flag from US-234a).

userStories.title: Real-action NAR golden corpus for every Wave-10 ruleset (consolidate script-builders)
userStories.description: tests/integration/test_nar_regression_corpus.py byte-pins terminal_result/terminal_reason/event_count/final_event_hash/event_hash_chain for the six Wave-10 rulesets ONLY as all-NOOP DRAW games (NoopMockAdapter via _canonical_draw_factory/_bench10_draw); only mini7 has a real-action scripted game. So the tier-ordered NAR interactions (kill/protect/roleblock/ninja/track/watch/clean/investigate) have NO real-action golden - a refactor that breaks resolution ordering would still pass. Add real-action full-game goldens (scripted DeterministicMockAdapter games that actually drive each ruleset's signature night actions) for every Wave-10 ruleset, pinning terminal_result + final event_hash + chain digest. Consolidate the drifting conftest script-builders (make_villager_script / make_mafia_script / make_town_win_script / make_mafia_win_script) into one reusable, role-aware builder.
acceptanceCriteria: For each of deception13_v1, roleblock10_v1, visit12_v1, ninja13_v1, sk12_v1, jester8_v1 there is at least one REAL-ACTION scripted full game (DeterministicMockAdapter driving that ruleset's signature actions - e.g. roleblock denies a power role, ninja suppresses a tracked kill, SK solo kill, jester lynch-bait) whose terminal_result + final event_hash + event_hash_chain digest are byte-pinned; a NOOP-DRAW-only game is NOT sufficient for these.

A test asserts each real-action golden's pinned terminal_result + final event_hash + chain digest are stable across a re-run (replay-determinism) and that the resolution exercised the intended NAR tier (e.g. the roleblock game's blocked-action feedback is present). tests/integration/test_nar_regression_corpus.py uses mock adapters (DeterministicMockAdapter/NoopMockAdapter) and already runs under the default pytest -m 'not integration' gate; the integration marker is reserved for tests that hit real LLM providers (pyproject.toml). The new real-action goldens MUST NOT add pytest.mark.integration so they remain in the default gate.

The drifting per-script builders in conftest are consolidated into ONE reusable role-aware script builder used by the corpus; a test (or the corpus itself) demonstrates the four old builders' behaviors are reproducible through the consolidated builder.
Pure/deterministic: no wall-clock/unseeded-random; replays through the event log.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Real adapters: llm/mock.DeterministicMockAdapter (script keyed by (phase_id, public_player_id)). Existing all-NOOP factories: _bench10_draw/_canonical_draw_factory use NoopMockAdapter. Consolidate conftest make_*_script helpers. tests/integration/test_nar_regression_corpus.py uses mock adapters and already runs under the default pytest -m 'not integration' gate (it carries ZERO pytest.mark.integration; the integration marker means 'hits real LLM providers', pyproject.toml). The new real-action goldens MUST NOT add pytest.mark.integration so they stay in the default gate.

userStories.title: Hot-path secondary indexes migration 0049 (game_events / llm_calls / retention)
userStories.description: Hot read paths lack secondary indexes: game_events has only the (game_id, sequence) unique constraint (no standalone game_id index for full-chain reads), and llm_calls has only FKs (no composite index on (game_id, agent_build_id, event_id), and retention-scan columns are unindexed). Add the hot-path secondary indexes as ONE linear Alembic migration 0049 (after the current head 0048). Idempotent up/down; no data migration.
acceptanceCriteria: A new Alembic migration 0049 (down_revision 0048) adds: an index on game_events.game_id; a composite index on llm_calls (game_id, agent_build_id, event_id); and indexes on the retention-scan columns used by the retention sweep (identify them from the retention code and index the actual scan predicate columns).
The migration is linear after head 0048 (a test/assert confirms a single head after this migration) and has a working downgrade that drops exactly the added indexes.
The corresponding Index/__table_args__ declarations are added to the SQLAlchemy models so models and migration agree (a metadata-vs-migration consistency check passes).
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Current head is 0048_game_mirror_pair_metadata. Sites: db/models.GameEvent (uq_game_event_sequence only) + db/models.LlmCall (FKs only). Find the retention sweep code to identify the real scan columns before indexing them.

userStories.title: Isolate child-game failures in the scheduler gather (return_exceptions)
userStories.description: runner/scheduler.py runs all child games of a gauntlet via asyncio.gather(*(_run_one_game(...) for child in child_games)) at ~line 254 with NO return_exceptions, so a single game's exception aborts the entire gauntlet (and cancels its siblings). Make the gather non-isolating-proof: collect per-game results/exceptions (return_exceptions=True), let surviving games complete, and record which child games failed so the gauntlet finishes with partial results instead of crashing. This is the prerequisite for the FAILED status + bounded retry in US-241.
acceptanceCriteria: The scheduler gather no longer aborts the whole gauntlet on one child-game failure: failures are captured per-game (return_exceptions=True or equivalent) and surviving games complete; a test injects a failing _run_one_game and asserts the other child games still complete and the failure is recorded, not propagated as a gauntlet-level crash.
Per-game failures are logged with the failing game_id (structured); a test asserts the failing game is identifiable from the result.
No change to the success path's rating/ingestion behavior for the games that DID complete (assert completed games still produce their normal outcomes).
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Site: runner/scheduler.py ~line 254 asyncio.gather over child_games (no return_exceptions). Single-host resilience only; no campaign abstraction. Pairs with US-241.

userStories.title: FAILED game status + bounded single-host retry
userStories.description: There is no FAILED game status (game status strings observed: CREATED/PENDING/RUNNING/COMPLETED; the enum has no FAILED). With per-game failures now isolated (US-240), give a failed child game a terminal FAILED status and a bounded single-host retry (retry up to N times, then mark FAILED) so a transient failure does not silently lose a game and a permanent failure is visibly terminal. No campaign abstraction, no distributed retry - a simple bounded in-process retry on the single-host scheduler.
acceptanceCriteria: A FAILED game status is added and persisted; a child game that exhausts its bounded retry is recorded as FAILED (terminal), distinct from COMPLETED; a test asserts a permanently-failing game ends in FAILED after the bounded attempts.

A bounded single-host retry re-runs a failed child game up to a configurable max-attempts (settings-driven); a test asserts a game that fails once then succeeds ends COMPLETED with the retry counted, and a game that always fails ends FAILED after exactly max-attempts.
A FAILED game writes ZERO rating rows (it never reached a valid terminal_result, so _should_apply_ratings already excludes it); a test asserts a FAILED game produces no rating writes.

FAILED is added as a string literal to the existing game-status string set - status remains a String column (db/models.py:152, no DB enum migration). Define the FAILED literal ONCE in a shared constant/Literal used by the scheduler, the status writes, and any terminal-status query; assert no other module hardcodes the FAILED string.

Audit the existing COMPLETED-only predicates so a FAILED row is never mistaken for terminal - ratings/model_rollup.py:66 (_COMPLETED_STATUS) / ratings/judge_sampling.py:138 / runner/scheduler.py:126 / runner/continuous_matchmaking.py still exclude FAILED games; a test asserts a FAILED game appears in none of those COMPLETED-scoped results.

FAILED games are excluded from scheduler/matchmaker re-selection: audit the scheduler.py status!='COMPLETED' re-pick (~L126) + continuous_matchmaking so a FAILED row is never re-run; a test asserts a FAILED game is not re-selected.
Retry is in-process single-host only (no distributed/durable retry queue, no campaign abstraction).
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Game status is a bare String column today (db/models.py:152, also 184/286; no enum FAILED). Add FAILED via a single shared literal/constant - COMPLETED is currently a scattered literal (judge_sampling.py:138, scheduler.py:126, model_rollup.py:66). _should_apply_ratings already requires terminal_result in {TOWN,MAFIA,DRAW}, so FAILED is naturally unrated. Builds on US-240's isolated gather. No enum migration.

userStories.title: Single-host Postgres backup runbook + tested hash-chain-verifying restore
userStories.description: Add a single-host Postgres backup/restore runbook and a TESTED restore that re-verifies the event-log hash-chain after restore, so a restored database is provably intact (no silent chain corruption). No distributed/HA backup - a single-host dump/restore runbook with a verification step. (The DB-default-driver flip is deliberately OUT of scope: flipping the production default + adding a settings assertion is a config-behavior change beyond this wave's surface/ranked/single-host-hardening remit and risks destabilizing the sqlite-based default test gate; it may be mentioned only as a docs note in the runbook.)
acceptanceCriteria: A documented backup runbook (dump) + restore procedure for the single-host Postgres exists (a runbook doc the story is explicitly allowed to create) covering the production compose stack.

A tested restore: a test (or scripted harness invoked by a test) loads a backup of a finished game's events into a fresh DB and re-verifies the hash-chain (event_hash = sha256(prev + canonical_json(event))) across the restored game_events, asserting the chain is unbroken and matches the pre-backup final hash.
Single-host only - no HA/replication/distributed backup.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: This story is explicitly permitted to create a runbook doc (the only doc-creating story in this wave). Hash-chain rule: event_hash = sha256(prev_event_hash + canonical_json(event_without_hash_or_timestamp)). Verify the chain re-validates post-restore. The Postgres-as-non-dev-default flip is OUT of scope (scope drift; may appear only as a docs note in the runbook).

userStories.title: Add the bench10_v1 ruleset to the public ladder context (ladder no longer mini7-only)
userStories.description: The public ladder page hardcodes RULESETS = ['mini7_v1'] (web/dashboard/src/routes/(public)/ladder/+page.svelte) and the home/profile surfaces pin mini7_v1, so the scientific bench10_v1 (and any other registered canonical ruleset) is never ladder-visible even though it is rated. Generalize the public ladder selection to surface every CANONICAL_TEAM ruleset the leaderboard API exposes, not a single hardcoded id, so the canonical benchmark ladders are all reachable. Keep canonical-only here (experimental contexts stay on the multi-context leaderboard from US-229).
acceptanceCriteria: The public ladder selection is driven by the canonical rulesets the leaderboard API exposes (sourced from get_ruleset/BUILTIN_RULESET_IDS + the CANONICAL_TEAM contexts), not a hardcoded RULESETS=['mini7_v1']; consume the canonical ruleset list from the US-226 ruleset-list endpoint rather than re-deriving or hardcoding it; an e2e test asserts at least mini7_v1 and bench10_v1 are selectable on the ladder.
Switching the ladder ruleset shows that ruleset's canonical standings (the per-ruleset leaderboard); a test asserts the ladder reflects the selected canonical ruleset.
Only CANONICAL_TEAM rulesets appear on the ladder (experimental PLACEMENT/SOLO_RATE stay on the US-229 multi-context leaderboard); a test asserts a non-canonical context is not offered on the ladder.
Existing ladder anonymity assertions still pass.
pnpm -C web/dashboard lint && check && test:e2e pass.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Sites: web/dashboard/src/routes/(public)/ladder/+page.svelte RULESETS=['mini7_v1'] + home/profile mini7 pins. Depends on US-228 so the per-faction/per-ruleset card data is available. Complements US-226 (lobby) and US-229 (multi-context leaderboard).
